hero.title: Check whether a parcel fits the use you have in mind.
hero.summary: Enter a parcel, choose a market and target use, and get a quick fit readout. If the site looks promising, request a paid evaluation.

visual.title: Quick parcel screen for early land decisions
visual.text: The public result shows fit band, key reasons, and parcel facts. Paid follow-up adds deeper diligence and site-specific next steps.

brief.title: A fast screen, not a full underwriting memo.
brief.text: Use it to decide whether a parcel deserves more time.

screenHeading.title: Try a parcel and see what the screen returns.
screenHeading.text: Enter a parcel ID, market, and target use. The screen returns a suggested use, fit band, and the reasons behind it.

stageHeader.title: Test the parcel.
stageHeader.text: Use the tool to see whether the site looks promising enough to justify deeper work.

overviewHeading.title: It helps you decide whether to keep going.
overviewHeading.text: This is an early screen for land opportunities, not a final investment recommendation.

overview.title: Screen fast
overview.text: Rule out weak parcels before spending more time on utilities, entitlement, or outreach.

overview.title: See the reason
overview.text: Get the likely use, fit band, and main reasons in plain language.

overview.title: Escalate when it is worth it
overview.text: Send promising sites into LeCrown's paid evaluation flow with the parcel context already attached.

signalHeading.title: What the screen found on this parcel.
signalHeading.text: Below are the parcel ID, market, acreage, suggested use, and fit band from the current screen.

workflowHeading.title: From quick screen to paid evaluation.
workflowHeading.text: Strong parcels can move forward without re-entering the same context from scratch.

workflow.title: Screen the parcel
workflow.text: Enter the parcel ID, market, and target mode to generate the first fit signal.

workflow.title: Review the surfaced reasons
workflow.text: Use the fit band, site facts, and readout cards to decide whether the parcel merits more attention.

workflow.title: Request formal evaluation
workflow.text: Capture the parcel, package, and project context so LeCrown can scope the next layer of work.

workflow.title: Advance into diligence
workflow.text: Strong parcels can move into scorecards, blocker review, operator memos, and advisory follow-up.

audienceHeading.title: Useful across brokerage, development, and acquisition roles.
audienceHeading.text: Different teams look at the same parcel from different angles. The page supports all of them without pretending the first screen is full diligence.

audiences.title: Developers and sponsors
audiences.text: Triage land before spending serious time on entitlement, utility, or operator conversations.

audiences.title: Brokers and site selectors
audiences.text: Give prospects a concrete first-pass readout and a clean path into formal evaluation.

audiences.title: Investors and acquisition teams
audiences.text: Compare parcels using a consistent frame before committing deeper diligence budget.

audiences.title: Operators and advisors
audiences.text: Carry the parcel context into the next conversation instead of rebuilding the story from zero.

faqHeading.title: What teams usually want to know before using the screen.
faqHeading.text: These answers clarify the boundary between the instant screen and the paid evaluation work behind it.

faq.question: Is the parcel screen the final diligence product?
faq.answer: No. The first screen is intentionally concise. It is built for triage, not for final investment or site decisions.

faq.question: What happens when I request a paid evaluation?
faq.answer: LeCrown receives the parcel context, fit readout, selected package, and project note so the next step can be scoped around the site.

faq.question: Does the page call GridScope directly from the browser?
faq.answer: No. The browser talks only to LeCrown's backend. GridScope credentials remain on the server side.

faq.question: Can this run without live GridScope credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The interface falls back to realistic sample data when the live environment is not configured, so the page remains usable during setup.

ctaBanner.title: Move a promising parcel into LeCrown's paid diligence flow.
ctaBanner.text: If a site looks viable, send it through with package intent and project context so the next layer of work starts with the right parcel and the right questions.
